questions.q: A pen is bought for ₹50 and sold for ₹65. What is the profit percentage?
If an item is sold at a loss of 20% for ₹800, what was its cost price?
A product marked ₹500 sold at 20% discount. Selling price is:
Two items sold at same SP. One at 20% profit, one at 20% loss. Overall result:
questions.explanation: Equal profit/loss% always gives loss of (20/10)² = 4%. Total cost > total SP.
